commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: And he arose from there, and came into the regions of Judea beyond the Jordan: and the people came unto him again; and, as his custom was, he taught them again. Cornelius a Lapide Christ disputeth with the Pharisees touching divorcement: 13blesseth the children that are brought unto him: 17 resolveth a rich man how he may inherit life everlasting: 23telleth his disciples of the danger of riches: 28 promiseth rewards to them that forsake anything for the gospel: 32foretelleth his death and resurrection: 35 biddeth the two ambitious suitors to think rather of suffering with him: 46 and restoreth to Bartimus his sight.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
